mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so a sustained downtrend in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is stable and fails if rate cuts are accompanied by acute recession signals.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of windows where FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in a rolling 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST signals improving housing demand and construction activity, which lifts cyclically sensitive earnings expectations and supports equity prices over the next quarter. The signal is bearish or neutral when the housing surge is driven by inventory distortion rather than true end-demand.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive 60-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average excess return relative to untiggered periods is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates slowing labor income growth and weaker final demand, which should pressure corporate revenues and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when unemployment rises only because labor force participation expands while payrolls remain strong.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or if the sign of the average forward return is not negative after unemployment shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, payroll growth confirms broad labor-market strength and supports household income, retail spending, and equity earnings expectations. The joint condition predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days and fails if payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of matched observations.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO indicates industrial production acceleration and better utilization of the corporate operating base, which should lift cyclical earnings and market returns over the next month. The effect fails if output gains occur alongside demand destruction or inventory liquidation.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the hit rate is not above 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger gap between CPIAUCSL inflation and FEDFUNDS implies real policy is becoming more restrictive, compressing equity valuations and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should be strongest when inflation is rising faster than policy rates and fails when both move down together.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising inflation and unchanged/higher FEDFUNDS do not show below-baseline SPX returns over 20 trading days, or if the joint signal has Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS signals weakening retail sales and immediate household demand, which should reduce near-term revenue expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next week. The signal fails when sales softness is driven by price deflation rather than real volume weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS surprises are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the average 5-day return is not below zero in triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is slowing while PAYEMS remains weak, the combination indicates broad demand deceleration rather than a temporary sectoral shock, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP weakness is offset by a rapid recovery in payrolls or by an external reacceleration in housing and spending.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-down/PAYEMS-weak condition does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of occurrences, or if the conditional mean return is not materially below unconditional baseline.
